features.name: Project scaffold
features.notes: Git repo initialized with .gitignore, .env.example, and docker-compose.yml per foundation plan Task 1. Directories created. No sensors applicable yet — repo has no application code. NEXT_PUBLIC_BETTER_AUTH_URL intentionally added early as a harmless placeholder needed by the Better Auth client config in Task 7. QA approved with reservations; required corrections completed. Security verdict: ADVISORY only. Follow-ups: runtime rejection of placeholder secrets and broader env-file ignore coverage before deployment work.

features.name: Nginx config
features.notes: Nginx reverse-proxy config at nginx/nginx.conf. Proxies / to next:3000 with proper headers. FastAPI not publicly routed. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Residual risk: local nginx syntax validation deferred until tooling/runtime is available.

features.name: PostgreSQL schema migration
features.notes: Initial schema created at database/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql. 5 tables: words, cards, review_log, ai_content_cache, user_settings with CHECK, UNIQUE, REFERENCES constraints and 3 indexes for hot query paths. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Follow-ups: enforce review_log.user_id consistency with cards.user_id; define cleanup for soft Better Auth user references; verify FSRS due serialization; later decide provider allowlist and AI cache expiry policy.

features.name: Hebrew seed data and import script
features.notes: Top-20 Biblical Hebrew words in database/seed/words.csv with idempotent importer (seed_words.py). Uses ON CONFLICT (hebrew) DO NOTHING against UNIQUE hebrew column, parameterized queries, DATABASE_URL from env. Default CSV path resolved relative to script location. Connection cleanup via try/finally. Initial QA rejected dead-code idempotency; correction added UNIQUE words.hebrew plus ON CONFLICT (hebrew), fixed the duplicated erets gloss, made the default CSV path script-relative, and added explicit cleanup. QA recheck: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN.

features.name: FastAPI stub
features.notes: Minimal FastAPI stub at backend/. Health check only. Dependencies: fastapi==0.136.1, uvicorn[standard]==0.46.0. Dockerfile uses python:3.14-slim. LiteLLM excluded per ADR-001. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Container runtime validation deferred because Docker Compose is unavailable locally.

features.name: Next.js scaffold
features.notes: Next.js 16.2.6 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui (button/card/input/label). Better Auth deps installed. Dockerfile uses node:24-alpine. output: standalone. Build and lint green. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Pre-Task 7 follow-ups: remove or justify @better-auth/client pre-alpha dependency, move shadcn to devDependencies, update frontend/CLAUDE.md to reference root contract, and consider non-root runner user before deployment.

features.name: Better Auth configuration
features.notes: Better Auth 1.6.11 configured. Server config in src/lib/auth.ts (PostgreSQL pool, email+password). Browser client in src/lib/auth-client.ts (better-auth/client stable subpath). Catch-all route at src/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts. @better-auth/client removed (redundant pre-alpha). shadcn moved to devDeps. frontend/CLAUDE.md links root contract. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS; doc corrections completed. Security verdict: CLEAN. Better Auth DB migration deferred until PostgreSQL is available.

features.name: Auth pages
features.notes: Login/register page at frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx. Combined mode toggle (login/register), email+password fields, name field for register, error and loading state, redirects to / on success. Uses authClient.signIn.email and authClient.signUp.email. shadcn Card/Input/Label/Button components. Build and lint green. QA corrections: mode toggle clears stale error; error.message uses ?? fallback. End-to-end flow deferred — Better Auth tables not yet migrated. Follow-up before foundation-010: map raw Better Auth error strings to safe Portuguese messages instead of forwarding server text verbatim. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS; corrections completed (clear stale errors on mode toggle; safe undefined-message fallback). Security verdict: ADVISORY. Before foundation-010, replace raw Better Auth error forwarding with safe user-facing Portuguese messages to reduce user-enumeration/info-leak risk.

features.name: Route protection and dashboard shell
features.notes: Route protection now uses frontend/src/proxy.ts, which protects all routes except /login and /api/auth. It checks better-auth session cookies and redirects to /login if missing. Dashboard uses server-side auth.api.getSession. Build and lint pass without the deprecated middleware warning.

features.name: Full stack smoke test
features.notes: Docker Compose smoke test verified live. Added 002_seed_words.sql to auto-seed words via initdb.d. Fixed docker-compose.yml postgres volume path from /var/lib/postgresql/data to /var/lib/postgresql (postgres:18+ convention change). Smoke results: nginx/Next.js → 307 (proxy middleware active), FastAPI /health → {status:ok}, PostgreSQL COUNT(words) → 20. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Follow-ups carried into core-engine: Better Auth tables still require npx better-auth migrate before auth E2E coverage; NEXT_PUBLIC_BETTER_AUTH_URL must be passed as a Docker build arg before non-local deployment; docker-entrypoint-initdb.d seeds only on first named-volume initialization; add explicit secret-handling guidance for AI provider keys before real AI calls; Better Auth raw error forwarding still needs safe Portuguese message mapping.

features.name: Backend infrastructure
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 1. Created backend/db.py (psycopg2 ThreadedConnectionPool, FastAPI dependency, commit/rollback/putconn lifecycle). Created backend/models.py (Word, CardWithContent, NextCardsResponse, ReviewRequest, ReviewResponse — pure Pydantic contracts). Created backend/tests/__init__.py and backend/tests/conftest.py (mock_db and client fixtures with dependency_overrides). Added to requirements.txt: psycopg2-binary==2.9.10, pytest==8.3.4, httpx==0.28.1, pytest-mock==3.14.0. Updated Dockerfile to add build-essential + libpq-dev (psycopg2-binary has no pre-built wheel for Python 3.14 and must be compiled from source). Docker build: PASS. Import smoke test (db_connection, all models): PASS. pytest: 0 collected (conftest only — scaffolding only, no real test modules yet). QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Hardening follow-up completed: backend/.dockerignore now excludes .env, .env.*, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .pytest_cache/ and related Python/OS junk; follow-up QA verdict: APPROVED; Security verdict: CLEAN. Carry forward: constrain format/rating before core-003; add lock guard around _get_pool() before core-004; defer multi-stage image, non-root user, explicit DATABASE_URL validation, and pydantic pin to pre-production hardening.

features.name: FSRS scheduling service
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 2. Created backend/fsrs_service.py: fsrs_state_to_card, card_to_fsrs_state, schedule_review, determine_format. Created backend/tests/test_fsrs_service.py: 8 tests, all passing. Added fsrs==6.3.1 to requirements.txt (PyPI package name is 'fsrs', not 'py-fsrs'; v6 has no 'reps' field — 'reps' is carried as a synthetic counter in our state dict and set as a dynamic attribute on Card, which is a dataclass without __slots__). TDD cycle confirmed in Docker: red on ImportError, green on all 8. Full suite 8/8, 0 regressions. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Carry forward: before core-003, constrain CardWithContent.format and ReviewRequest.rating, and guard fsrs_state_to_card() so missing keys do not overwrite FSRS defaults with None; before core-004, make _get_pool() thread-safe, prevent invalid rating from surfacing as an unhandled KeyError, and validate fsrs_state shape before datetime parsing.

features.name: AI content generation service
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 3. Created backend/ai_service.py: hash_prompt (SHA-256, 16-char hex, keyed by word_id+format+provider_name), _build_prompt (format-specific Portuguese prompts), generate_content (calls provider.generate, parses JSON response). Provider boundary is a _Provider placeholder with a .generate() interface — real SDK wrappers deferred until first needed. Created backend/tests/test_ai_service.py: 5 tests with mocked provider (patch ai_service.provider.generate). Carry-forward constraints closed in this slice: models.py CardWithContent.format now Literal['multiple_choice','flashcard','typing']; ReviewRequest.rating now Literal[1,2,3,4]; fsrs_service.py fsrs_state_to_card() now uses explicit key-presence guards to prevent partial dicts from overwriting FSRS defaults with None. No new dependencies added. Full suite 13/13 PASS, 0 regressions. Pre-core-004 hardening also completed: ReviewRequest.format_used constrained to Literal; _build_prompt raises ValueError for unknown formats (no fallthrough); generate_content wraps json.loads into structured ValueError on JSONDecodeError; _get_pool thread-safe via double-checked locking (threading.Lock); 2 new error-path tests added. Full suite 15/15 PASS. Remaining open item: fsrs_state datetime shape validation before datetime.fromisoformat() — add at router layer in core-004. 2026-05-26/27 production follow-up: Anthropic/Claude is now wired as the first real provider via anthropic==0.104.1, with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and optional `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` env support. Default model is `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`. `hash_prompt()` was versioned to `v2` so stale placeholder Claude cache entries are bypassed after deploy. `docker-compose.yml` now attaches `fastapi` to both `internal` and `external` networks so provider SDK calls have outbound internet access without exposing FastAPI publicly. Backend pytest in container: 41/41 PASS. Live public verification confirmed real Claude-generated session content.

features.name: Session router
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 4. Created backend/session_router.py: GET /session/next-cards (fetches due cards + new words, generates/caches AI content, returns NextCardsResponse) and POST /session/review (validates ownership, updates FSRS state, logs review, returns ReviewResponse). Router registered in main.py via app.include_router. fsrs_state validated at router layer via _validate_fsrs_state() — type check + ISO datetime check for due/last_review fields; returns 422 with coherent detail on failure. Created backend/tests/test_session_router.py: 7 tests (happy paths for both routes, missing header 422, ownership 403, card not found 404, invalid fsrs_state type 422, invalid fsrs_state datetime 422). Full suite 22/22 PASS, 0 regressions. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Deferred router-layer validation requirement from earlier slices is closed. Carry forward: before core-009, guard NULL daily_new_limit / preferred_provider fallback, catch invalid DB format_override as a structured error, and constrain ReviewRequest.response_time_ms. Before any external exposure, replace trusted X-User-ID header identity and remove internal Python type names from fsrs_state validation errors. AI provider calls are mocked via cache hits in tests; real provider wiring deferred.

features.name: Session proxy routes
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 5. Created frontend/src/app/api/session/next-cards/route.ts (GET — Better Auth session check, 401 if unauthenticated, forwards to FastAPI with X-User-ID from session.user.id) and frontend/src/app/api/session/review/route.ts (POST — same auth pattern, forwards JSON body). Created frontend/src/lib/api.ts: typed interfaces (Word, CardWithContent, NextCardsResponse, ReviewRequest, ReviewResponse) and helpers (getNextCards, submitReview) that call the proxy routes. Sensors: npm run lint — clean; npm run build — compiled successfully, both routes appear as dynamic (ƒ) in route tree. BETTER_AUTH_SECRET warning is pre-existing, not introduced here. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Browser-to-backend trust boundary is now mediated by the server-side Better Auth session; the browser no longer controls X-User-ID. Before core-009, add cache:no-store to the next-cards upstream fetch, wrap malformed request.json() as 400, wrap upstream fetch/json failures as structured 502/503, and wire FASTAPI_URL=http://fastapi:8000 for the Next.js container runtime. Before external exposure, stop transparently forwarding raw FastAPI error payloads and add an explicit review POST body-size guard. No frontend test framework present; no tests added per project convention.

features.name: HebrewWord component
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 6. Created frontend/src/components/HebrewWord.tsx. Props: text, showNiqqud (default true), size (sm/md/lg/xl, default md), className. Renders <span dir='rtl' lang='he'> with Tailwind size classes. Internal stripNiqqud() strips niqqud and cantillation Unicode ranges when showNiqqud is false. Uses cn() from @/lib/utils — no new dependencies. Sensors: lint clean; build compiled successfully; tsc --noEmit errors are pre-existing stale .next/types artifacts unrelated to this slice. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Non-blocking follow-up: clean stale .next/types artifacts before core-007 so TypeScript signal is useful again. core-007 unblocked.

features.name: Exercise UI components
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 7. Created frontend/src/components/RatingBar.tsx (4-button FSRS rating bar, color-coded), SessionProgress.tsx (progress text + bar, total===0 safe), and ExerciseCard.tsx (client component with MultipleChoiceExercise, FlashcardExercise, TypingExercise sub-renderers). Options are shuffled once on mount via useState initializer to avoid react-hooks/purity violation. Sub-components keyed by card_id for automatic state reset on new card. Uses HebrewWord, shadcn Card/Button/Input, no new dependencies. Sensors: tsc --noEmit clean (stale .next artifacts removed first), lint clean, build compiled successfully. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Non-blocking follow-ups: add type="button" to non-submit buttons and a visible fallback for unknown card.format during or before core-008 integration. core-008 unblocked.

features.name: Session page
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 8. Created frontend/src/app/session/page.tsx: client component implementing loading/active/empty/complete/error state machine. Loads cards via getNextCards(), renders current card with ExerciseCard and progress with SessionProgress, submits ratings via submitReview() with response_time_ms, advances through queue, moves to complete when exhausted. Submit failures are non-fatal (logged, session continues). Updated frontend/src/app/page.tsx to add Iniciar sessão link to /session. Button.asChild not supported by base-ui Button — used styled Link elements instead. useRef initialized with 0 (not Date.now()) to satisfy react-hooks/purity rule. Sensors: tsc --noEmit clean, lint clean, build compiled successfully — /session appears as static shell in route tree. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Before core-009: apply proxy hardening from core-005, disable duplicate rating taps while submitReview() is in flight, bound response_time_ms server-side before analytics use, and fix session completion/progress assumptions if session_size can differ from cards.length. core-009 unblocked.

features.name: End-to-end smoke test
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 9. Hardened proxy routes (cache:no-store on next-cards GET; 400 on malformed review body; 503 on upstream network failure; 502 on upstream JSON parse failure — no raw error details forwarded in those paths). FASTAPI_URL default added to docker-compose.yml (${FASTAPI_URL:-http://fastapi:8000}). Duplicate review submissions guarded via submitting state in session page + ratingDisabled prop threaded into ExerciseCard/RatingBar. response_time_ms bounded ge=0 le=300_000 in backend/models.py. AI placeholder fallback added to ai_service.generate_content (catches NotImplementedError, returns deterministic content from word data so smoke path works without a real API key). ExerciseCard: ratingDisabled prop added, type=button on all non-submit buttons, Portuguese strings harmonized, unknown-format fallback card added. Session page: reviewedCount tracks actual reviewed cards (vs session_size). Backend: 3 new placeholder tests added; 25/25 pass. Stack smoke: all 4 containers healthy; FastAPI GET /session/next-cards returned 5 cards with placeholder AI content; POST /session/review returned next_due, new_stability, new_difficulty, new_reps. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Better Auth tables not yet migrated — browser-level auth flow not verified in this smoke. core-engine is complete for local development; next track is dashboard-deploy.

features.name: Daily stats endpoint
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 1. Created backend/stats_router.py: GET /stats/daily returns reviews_today, new_words_today, retention_rate (30-day window, 0.0 if no reviews), streak_days (terminal consecutive daily-review run ending at the most recent review date). X-User-ID required via Header; missing produces 422. All SQL parameterized. DailyStats Pydantic response model. Router registered in main.py. Created backend/tests/test_stats_router.py: 3 tests (happy path all fields, missing header 422, new user zeros including retention_rate==0.0). Full suite 28/28 PASS, 0 regressions. Frontend lint and build green. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Carry-forward: add an explicit test that documents non-zero streak when the latest review was yesterday; add a FastAPI-side length bound for X-User-ID before external exposure; confirm streak SQL and timezone boundary behavior against live Postgres 18 data during dash-009.

features.name: Settings endpoint
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 2. Created backend/settings_router.py: GET /settings (reads user_settings, returns defaults if no row) and PUT /settings (upserts via ON CONFLICT, validates preferred_provider against allowed set). X-User-ID required via Header; missing produces 422. daily_new_limit bounded (ge=1, le=500) via Field; timezone validated via zoneinfo.ZoneInfo + max length 64. Pydantic field_validator rejects non-allowed providers and invalid timezones. Validators also applied on DB read-back as defense-in-depth. Created backend/tests/test_settings_router.py: 11 tests (GET defaults, GET saved values, PUT upsert, invalid provider 422, missing header 422 GET, missing header 422 PUT, SQL shape regression, daily_new_limit=0 422, daily_new_limit=-1 422, invalid timezone 422, overly long timezone 422). Full suite 39/39 PASS, 0 regressions. Frontend lint and build green. Allowed providers: claude, gpt-4o, gemini, ollama. Carry-forward: X-User-ID length bound should be added before external exposure; field_validator catch on DB read-back means a stored invalid provider or timezone would cause a 500 — add DB CHECK constraint on user_settings.preferred_provider and user_settings.timezone before dash-009.

features.name: Stats and settings proxy routes
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 3. Created frontend/src/app/api/stats/daily/route.ts (GET — Better Auth session check, 401 if unauthenticated, proxies to FastAPI /stats/daily with X-User-ID, cache:no-store, 503/502 error wrapping). Created frontend/src/app/api/settings/route.ts (GET + PUT — same auth pattern; PUT also wraps malformed request.json() as 400). Extended frontend/src/lib/api.ts with DailyStats and UserSettings interfaces and getDailyStats(), getSettings(), updateSettings() helpers. Both routes appear as dynamic (ƒ) in the Next.js build route tree. Sensors: ruff 4 pre-existing (none in new code); mypy 5 pre-existing (none in new code); pytest 39/39 PASS; ESLint clean; build compiled successfully. dash-004 is unblocked.

features.name: Dashboard UI
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 4. Created frontend/src/components/DashboardStats.tsx: presentational 4-card stats grid (streak, reviews today, new words today, retention) using shadcn Card components; accepts DailyStats from @/lib/api. Replaced frontend/src/app/page.tsx with full dashboard: server component with Better Auth session check (redirects to /login), direct FastAPI stats fetch using FASTAPI_URL env var + X-User-ID (server-side pattern — avoids circular proxy call), non-blocking stats (null on error renders shell without stats), Hebrew greeting, settings link, session-start button. Sensors: ruff 4 pre-existing, mypy 5 pre-existing, pytest 39/39 PASS, ESLint clean, build compiled — / is dynamic ƒ. dash-005 is unblocked. Carry-forward: document the direct server-component FastAPI fetch pattern in architecture docs and keep it limited to validated session-derived user IDs.

features.name: Settings page
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 5. Created frontend/src/app/settings/page.tsx: client component with useEffect/useState loading settings on mount via getSettings(), persisting via updateSettings() on submit. Editable fields: preferred_provider (select with 4 provider options), daily_new_limit (number input, UI-clamped 1–50), show_niqqud (checkbox). Save button reflects saving/saved/error states. Back navigation via useRouter. All copy in Portuguese. Sensors: ESLint clean; build compiled — /settings appears as static shell (○). No backend changes. dash-006 is unblocked.

features.name: Hebrew typography
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 6. Added Noto_Serif_Hebrew from next/font/google in frontend/src/app/layout.tsx, exposed as CSS variable --font-hebrew. HebrewWord.tsx updated to use [font-family:var(--font-hebrew)] instead of font-serif. Also applied dash-005 QA cleanup in settings/page.tsx: disabled={saveState !== 'idle'} and explicit isNaN guard on daily_new_limit parseInt. Sensors: ESLint clean; build compiled — route tree unchanged. dash-007 is unblocked.

features.name: HTTPS Nginx configuration
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 7. nginx/nginx.conf: HTTP 80 server block now returns 301 to HTTPS; HTTPS 443 ssl server block added with LetsEncrypt cert paths (/etc/letsencrypt/live/hebrai.co/fullchain.pem and privkey.pem), proxy to next:3000 with X-Forwarded-For and X-Forwarded-Proto headers added. docker-compose.yml nginx service: added /etc/letsencrypt:/etc/letsencrypt:ro volume mount. Also completed dash-006 typography cleanup: replaced all three remaining font-serif Hebrew surfaces with [font-family:var(--font-hebrew)] in login/page.tsx and ExerciseCard.tsx (Input and correct-answer span). grep confirms zero font-serif references remain in frontend/src/. Sensors: ESLint clean; build compiled. Nginx config syntax not verifiable locally (no nginx binary); will be validated on first VPS deploy. dash-008 is unblocked.

features.name: VPS deployment scripts
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 8. Created deploy/setup-vps.sh (one-time Ubuntu 24.04 provisioning: installs Docker + Certbot, clones repo, obtains initial cert via standalone, installs renewal hooks) and deploy/deploy.sh (idempotent redeploy: pull, build, nginx -t gate in throwaway container, up -d, nginx reload, FastAPI health check). Both chmod +x; pass bash -n; shellcheck not installed locally. Certificate strategy decided: Certbot standalone authenticator for BOTH issuance and renewal (single authenticator avoids the standalone/webroot renewal-config mismatch). Renewal uses pre/post renewal-hooks that stop/start the nginx container (~5-15s downtime per ~60d renewal); Ubuntu certbot.timer drives scheduling, no cron added. Absorbed all dash-007 hardening: (1) HTTP->HTTPS redirect now uses literal canonical host https://hebrai.co (no Host-header reflection); (2) HSTS max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains on 443 block; (3) cert mounts narrowed to /etc/letsencrypt/live/hebrai.co + /etc/letsencrypt/archive/hebrai.co (both required — live symlinks resolve into archive); (4) explicit ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3 + Mozilla-intermediate ssl_ciphers + ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off; (5) http2 on (nginx 1.25+ syntax; stable-alpine is 1.27); (6) standalone strategy documented in HANDOFF/session-log; (7) N/A (no webroot); (8) nginx -t mandatory gate before stack changes in deploy.sh. Sensors: frontend npm run lint clean, npm run build compiled (no frontend changes this slice). Not verifiable locally: nginx config syntax (no nginx binary; would also need live certs) and both scripts run unaltered against a real VPS — first real execution is the de facto test. dash-009 is unblocked. QA correction pass: the post-deploy health check originally piped `docker compose exec -T fastapi curl ...`, but backend/Dockerfile (python:3.14-slim) does not install curl, so the check always failed even on a healthy app. Replaced with a python3 urllib.request stdlib one-liner run inside the fastapi container (tools already present in the image). bash -n deploy/deploy.sh passes.

features.name: Full end-to-end verification
features.notes: Dashboard/deploy plan Task 9. Verified live (Docker daemon started for this slice; compose plugin invoked directly at /Applications/Docker.app/Contents/Resources/cli-plugins/docker-compose because ~/.docker/cli-plugins symlink is a stale AppTranslocation path). VERIFIED: (1) backend pytest in container — 39/39 PASS; (2) frontend npm run build — compiled successfully; (3) full-stack smoke (postgres+fastapi+next; nginx excluded locally since cert mounts target absent /etc/letsencrypt) — FastAPI /health ok, PostgreSQL SELECT COUNT(*) FROM words = 20; (4) dash-008 health-check command run VERBATIM inside fastapi container -> printed 'ok' (in-practice confirmation, not inspection); (5) hardened nginx config validated via isolation nginx -t with throwaway self-signed cert; (6) runtime TLS proxy proven with standalone nginx on the compose network + temp cert: HTTPS request -> HTTP 307, Location: /login (proxy.ts middleware reached through TLS front door), Strict-Transport-Security header present, no upstream errors. FIX during this slice (verification-blocking, nginx.conf only): nginx resolves literal proxy_pass upstreams at config-parse time, so deploy.sh's pre-flight gate (docker compose run --rm --no-deps nginx nginx -t) would fail with 'host not found in upstream next' on a first deploy when next is not yet running. Replaced literal proxy_pass http://next:3000 with request-time resolution: resolver 127.0.0.11 valid=30s ipv6=off + set $upstream_next next:3000 + proxy_pass http://$upstream_next. Re-proved by re-running the isolation nginx -t with NO network and next not running -> now passes; runtime proxy re-confirmed (307/login/HSTS). deploy.sh unchanged. NOT VERIFIABLE LOCALLY (VPS-only, by inspection): real Let's Encrypt issuance + standalone challenge, renewal hooks, setup-vps.sh end-to-end, live/archive cert symlink resolution, deploy.sh nginx -t gate against the real cert mounts, and nginx over a real TLS cert chain. RESIDUALS: backend X-User-ID still trusted/unbounded; invalid stored provider/timezone can still 500 on backend GET (no DB CHECK constraints yet); daily_new_limit UI cap 50 vs backend 500; standalone renewal causes ~5-15s nginx downtime per ~60d. dashboard-deploy track is functionally complete for local development; production cutover depends on the VPS-only items above.

open_decisions: Confirm the initial Hebrew seed list size and source before production import.
Confirm the production AI provider priority order behind the internal provider adapter.
Confirm final VPS hostname and certificate issuance details before deploy work.
Decide how to enforce review_log.user_id consistency with cards.user_id before production data paths rely on it.
